message:
fix(transport): add RAII guards for connection slots and dispatch counters

The transport router had a resource leak risk where connection slots and
active dispatch counters could become permanently orphaned if a future
was cancelled (dropped) between acquire and release, or if a panic
occurred during dispatch. In an async runtime built around
cancel-correctness, this was a correctness gap — the very kind of
silent resource leak that asupersync is designed to prevent.

Changes:

- Add ConnectionGuard RAII type on Endpoint that automatically calls
  release_connection() on drop, ensuring connection slots are freed
  even on cancellation or panic during dispatch
- Add DispatchGuard RAII type on SymbolDispatcher that decrements
  active_dispatches on drop, preventing counter drift on cancelled
  dispatches
- Replace all manual acquire/release pairs with guard-based patterns
  in dispatch_unicast, dispatch_multicast, dispatch_broadcast, and
  dispatch_with_fallback
- Add acquire_connection_guard() convenience method on Endpoint
- Also migrates HashMap → BTreeMap and adds PartialOrd/Ord derives
  on EndpointId and RouteKey as part of the ongoing deterministic
  iteration order initiative

The explicit release_connection() calls are removed in favor of implicit
drop semantics, matching the two-phase effect pattern (reserve/commit)
that asupersync uses elsewhere for cancel-safe resource management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
